jurisdiction_and_domain: United Kingdom occupational health and safety guidance for arboriculture and work at height

license.name: Open Government Licence v3.0
license.scope: manually transformed eligible HSE Crown text only
license.preferred_acknowledgement: Contains public sector information published by the Health and Safety Executive and licensed under the Open Government Licence.
license.limitations: No blanket rights conclusion is made for logos, images, illustrations, SVG, video, multimedia, linked publications, legislation, standards, or third-party material. Every such component is excluded.
license.change_notice: Manually selected, paraphrased, reorganized, UK-occupationally scoped, and stripped of exact ratings, statutory intervals, devices, climbing construction, rescue steps, legal overclaims, and cross-domain certification.

entries.reason: Planning, supervision, system boundary, tree context, inspection, swing, and rescue-readiness concepts were manually transformed with UK occupational scope and operational exclusions.
Ground-team planning, rescue availability, communication, observation, line housekeeping, workload, and stop-and-reassess concepts were manually transformed.

Task-specific planning, competence, line and anchor roles, supplementary-anchor context, and rescue readiness were retained; configurations, techniques, devices, video, and linked works were excluded.
Used only to verify the current HSE routing relationship among lifting-equipment pages; navigation and link-title text were excluded.

Inspection layers, deterioration, exceptional events, competent-person judgment, impartiality, records, dangerous-defect communication, and withdrawal were transformed; exact intervals, testing, enforcement, standards, and legal fields were excluded.

Planning, organisation, supervision, competence, hierarchy, suitable equipment, inspection, maintenance, emergency planning, and rescue were transformed; statistics, devices, cases, and legal links were excluded.
Only the landing-page statement that generic fall-arrest inspection principles may apply to similar rope equipment was transformed; the linked PDF was excluded and not fetched.
Used only for rights provenance and component exclusions; contact details, print-product exclusions, and legal boilerplate were excluded.

word_count_method: Unicode regex [\w\u2019'-]+ over CORPUS.md; whitespace count recorded separately
category_scope_note: The hardpoint, rigging, and rescue mappings contain only UK occupational treework system boundaries, line and anchor roles, inspection, communication, reassessment, and readiness. They provide no tree, branch, anchor, hardpoint, load, rope, bondage, or human-suspension certification and no rescue procedure.

evidence_scope.source_context: current HSE summaries for United Kingdom occupational arboriculture, work at height, and lifting-equipment inspection
evidence_scope.retained_system_boundary: a person is a load and supporting, fixing, and anchoring attachments belong inside the lifting-system boundary
evidence_scope.retained_tree_context: species, age, condition, disease, foreseeable loading, movement, and decay as professional assessment dimensions rather than a capacity checklist
evidence_scope.retained_anchor_and_line_roles: two load-bearing anchors where possible, separately anchored working and safety lines, supplementary anchors as swing-control geometry, and common-mode caveats
evidence_scope.retained_ground_and_change_controls: job planning, continuous communication, observation, line housekeeping, workload sharing, public exclusion, and stop-and-reassess
evidence_scope.retained_assurance_layers: competent daily checks, recorded high-wear rope inspection, interim inspection, maintenance, thorough examination, traceable records, and withdrawal
evidence_scope.excluded_operations: anchor selection and construction, climbing, connection, ascent, descent, changeover, hauling, lowering, friction, cutting, rescue procedure, exact ratings, statutory intervals, device endorsements, and legal conclusions beyond source scope

component_status.rights_rule: OGL treatment applies only to eligible substantive HSE Crown text; logos, site chrome, images, illustrations, SVG, video, multimedia, linked publications, legislation, standards, and third-party material are excluded.

qa_derivation_policy.allowed: UK occupational scope, planning, supervision, task-specific competence, person-as-load and system-boundary reasoning, tree-condition uncertainty, anchor and line roles with common-mode caveats, ground communication, line management, stop-and-reassess, inspection layers, defect withdrawal, and rescue readiness without procedure
qa_derivation_policy.forbidden: URL, title, retrieval date, source-order, regulation number, legal citation, equipment rating, statutory interval, device, brand, knot, technique, climbing or anchor construction, rescue procedure, tree or hardpoint approval, bondage procedure, human-suspension approval, or component trivia

sampling_guidance.source_weighting: one HSE treework/lifting source-family document, not one document per web page, anchor role, inspection layer, or legal topic
sampling_guidance.recommended_unit: source-aware chunks that retain UK occupational context, system boundary, relevant role distinctions, uncertainty, reassessment, and no-certification boundary
sampling_guidance.avoid: isolating a two-anchor, supplementary-anchor, daily-check, interval, or rescue statement from its role, competence, source-domain, operational-exclusion, and no-transfer boundaries

document_disjoint_requirement: Assign all eight reviewed HSE pages and every derived Markdown or QA item to one source-family split before chunking or derivation.
protected_split_requirement: Exclude QA, training-projection, evaluation, validation, holdout, OOD, shadow, benchmark, probe, dataset, sealed-holdout, and protected-QA documents from every training layer.
